data.title: SlowMist Security Assessment: No Risk of Private Key or Mnemonic Phrase Transmission Found in OKX Wallet
data.description: According to official sources, blockchain security firm SlowMist recently released a security assessment report, conducting a special audit of OKX Wallet. The results showed that in the audited version, no sensitive information such as private keys or mnemonic phrases were found to be transmitted to external servers, and no risk of sensitive data leakage was detected overall. It is understood that this assessment focused on whether OKX Wallet had any issues with the external transmission of private keys and mnemonic phrases, and the results indicated that all relevant core information was processed locally. The current handling of private keys and mnemonic phrases has become central to wallet security, and such audit results provide users with a more direct security reference.

data.title: AI TRENDS | GoPlus Launches SafuSkill Launchpad for AI Skill Development
data.description:
GoPlus has announced the official launch of the SafuSkill Launchpad, a platform designed to support the issuance and monetization of AI Skills. According to Foresight News, this new feature integrates an AI Skill marketplace, a token launchpad, and a developer revenue system. It enables developers to issue Skill tokens and automatically connect to PancakeSwap liquidity, creating an on-chain revenue model based on transaction fees, with developers able to withdraw accumulated earnings at any time.

On the technical side, the SafuSkill Launchpad incorporates AgentGuard security scanning, GitHub developer verification, and the Flap Protocol's joint curve mechanism to provide security and price discovery capabilities. SafuSkill aims to transform AI Skills from tools into assets, building the AgentFi infrastructure to promote the development of the Skill Economy.

data.title: Polymarket's probability of a US-Iran ceasefire by May 31st has fallen below 40%, down 12% in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of a ceasefire between the US and Iran by May 31st has dropped significantly on Polymarket, falling 12% in 24 hours. The total trading volume for the "US-Iran ceasefire" event contract is currently close to $85 million. The contract's rules state that if the US and Iran reach a formal ceasefire agreement (defined as a public announcement and mutual agreement to cease direct military action) by a specified date (11:59 PM Eastern Time), the market will mark it as "yes"; otherwise, it will be "no." A "formal ceasefire agreement" requires explicit public confirmation from both the US and Iranian governments that they have agreed to cease military hostilities against each other, or overwhelming consensus confirmed through media reports. Previously, Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed that US Army Chief of Staff Randy George was about to resign. This personnel change occurred one day after Trump's national address on the Iran war. Several senior Army officers described George's dismissal as a "blow to the Army." They were angry and frustrated by the decision, believing it further exacerbated the long-standing feeling of being "besieged" among the Army's top brass. Odaily Seer continues to focus on the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Major Banks Forecast U.S. Wage Growth Trends
data.description:
According to Jin10, several major banks have released their forecasts for the U.S. average hourly wage growth. Citibank predicts an annual growth rate of 3.5%, while JPMorgan Chase and TD Securities both forecast 3.6%. Barclays, Capital Economics, and several others expect a 3.7% increase. HSBC, UBS, and BNP Paribas anticipate a growth rate of 3.8%, aligning with the previous value.

For the monthly wage growth, Citibank projects a modest increase of 0.1%, with JPMorgan Chase, Mizuho Bank, and TD Securities forecasting a 0.2% rise. Wells Fargo and Imperial Bank expect a 0.3% growth, while Barclays and several others align with this prediction. Goldman Sachs, HSBC, and Moody's Analytics also foresee a 0.3% increase.

Nomura Securities, UBS, and Sumitomo Mitsui project a 0.3% rise, while BNP Paribas and Bank of America anticipate a 0.4% growth. Standard Chartered and Helaba Bank forecast a 0.4% increase, with Lloyds Bank slightly higher at 0.43%. Reuters' survey indicates an expected monthly growth rate of 0.3%.

data.title: Bitcoin's Role as a Risk Indicator During Easter Market Closure
data.description: Bitcoin may serve as a real-time risk barometer while major markets, including those in the U.S., are closed for Easter. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin's ability to trade 24 hours a day allows it to react to geopolitical developments and oil price volatility over the weekend. However, due to low liquidity during weekends, any significant movement would need to extend into traditional markets upon their reopening on Monday to be considered reliable.

data.title: South Korean and French Presidents Discuss Middle East Crisis
data.description:
South Korean President Lee Jae-myung and French President Emmanuel Macron held talks at the Blue House in Seoul to discuss the international crisis triggered by the Middle East conflict. According to RTHK, during the extensive discussions, President Lee expressed concerns over the escalating conflict in the Middle East, which is causing global instability and affecting the world economy and energy sectors. He emphasized the importance of the meeting in fostering peace in the Middle East and stabilizing the energy supply chain.

President Macron highlighted the potential for enhanced cooperation between the two nations in the defense sector to help stabilize the Middle East situation. He stressed the importance of stopping violence and conflict in regions such as the Strait of Hormuz. Macron also expressed confidence that countries not seeking dominance and concerned about the current uncertainties could collaborate effectively.

The leaders also discussed strengthening bilateral cooperation. President Macron invited President Lee to attend the G7 Summit in France in June, an invitation that President Lee accepted.

data.title: A whale deposited 689.34 BTC, worth $46.17 million, into Binance.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited 689.34 BTC, worth $46.17 million, into Binance.

data.title: Iran Warns of Severe Retaliation Against Attacks on Infrastructure
data.description: Iran has issued a stern warning regarding potential attacks on its infrastructure. According to Odaily, a spokesperson for Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Central Command stated that any assault on Iranian bridges, power plants, or energy facilities would provoke a strong response targeting all U.S. and Israeli assets in the region and occupied territories. The retaliation would also extend to critical assets of U.S. allies and host countries, promising to be more intense and destructive than previous actions. The spokesperson emphasized that countries hosting U.S. military bases must ensure the withdrawal of American forces to maintain safety.

data.title: Canada to Regulate Stablecoin Issuers Under New Framework
data.description: Canada has announced a new regulatory framework in its 2025 budget and Bill C-15, which will bring fiat-pegged stablecoin issuers under the supervision of the Bank of Canada. According to NS3.AI, the framework mandates that stablecoin issuers maintain reserve backing, ensure par-value redemption, and adhere to data security and governance standards. The detailed rules are expected to be developed over a period of up to 18 months, starting early this year, with full implementation aimed for 2027.

data.title: Iran's Central Command under Khatnam Anbia: Any attack on bridges, power plants, or energy infrastructure will trigger Iranian retaliation.
data.description: According to Iran's Press TV, a spokesperson for Iran's Hatem Anbia Central Command stated that any attack on Iranian bridges, power plants, or energy infrastructure would trigger strikes against all US and Israeli assets in the region and occupied territories, including key assets of US allies and host countries—strikes that would be far more intense and destructive than ever before. The spokesperson added that countries hosting US military bases must force the withdrawal of US troops if they wish to maintain security. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Strategy's Preferred Stock Offering to Fund Bitcoin Purchase
data.description: Strategy is set to raise funds through its preferred stock, STRC, this week. According to BlockBeats, the anticipated capital from this offering is expected to facilitate the acquisition of 4,535 bitcoins. This move aligns with the company's ongoing strategy to increase its cryptocurrency holdings.

data.title: STRC expects to raise enough funds this week to purchase 4,535 BTC.
data.description: Strategy's Stretch STRC is expected to have raised enough funds this week to purchase 4,535 BTC, with a large-scale purchase scheduled for Monday. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: IMF Highlights Risks of Tokenized Finance to Global Financial System
data.description:
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has issued a warning regarding the potential risks posed by tokenized finance to the global financial system. According to NS3.AI, Tobias Adrian, in a recent note, outlined four primary risks associated with tokenized finance. These include challenges related to safe-money settlement, legal certainty, interoperability, and the need for consistent regulation.

The IMF's note also proposed a comprehensive five-pillar policy roadmap aimed at addressing these concerns. The roadmap emphasizes the importance of ensuring safe-money settlement, establishing legal certainty, enhancing interoperability, implementing consistent regulation, and developing central bank liquidity tools to support 24/7 markets.

This initiative reflects the IMF's proactive approach to managing the evolving landscape of tokenized finance and its potential implications for the stability of the global financial system.

data.title: Anta Digital Technology Begins Internal Testing of DTClaw
data.description: Anta Digital Technology has initiated internal testing for its new product, DTClaw, which is designed to significantly reduce token consumption by up to 50%. According to NS3.AI, DTClaw is equipped with over 100 professional skills and seamlessly integrates with various enterprise systems, including office, finance, sales, and marketing.

data.title: Crude oil prices hit a new all-time high, and Gate's multi-asset trading strategy remains among the industry leaders.
data.description: On April 3, the United States and Israel launched strikes against Iranian infrastructure, triggering market concerns about disruptions to energy supplies in the Middle East. As a result, international oil prices surged, with WTI crude oil rising nearly 15% intraday, and the settlement price breaking through $110 for the first time since 2022; spot Brent crude oil prices also broke through the $140 mark, reaching a new high since 2008. According to CoinGlass data, activity in oil-related derivatives increased significantly, with XTI contract trading volume increasing by approximately 79.24% to $28.73 million and open interest rising by approximately 4.37% to $20.76 million. Specifically, Gate.com ranked first globally in XTI contract trading volume (approximately $21.24 million) and number of trades (approximately 74,500), and second globally in XTI contract open interest ($9.1 million). Gate Contracts has taken the lead in crude oil, pioneering the commodity contract sector, covering XBRUSDT (Brent crude oil) and WTIUSDT (WTI crude oil) perpetual contract trading, providing 24/7 trading, USDT settlement and up to 100x leverage, helping users to conduct cross-market asset allocation and strategy deployment in volatile market conditions.

data.title: Thailand's Ministry of Commerce: Inflation Pressure Remains Under Control
data.description: According to Jin10, Thailand's Ministry of Commerce has stated that the current inflation pressure in the country is under control. The ministry's assessment comes amid global economic uncertainties and fluctuating market conditions. The government continues to monitor the situation closely to ensure economic stability and maintain consumer confidence. Efforts are being made to address any potential challenges that may arise in the future.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation has staked another $46.64 million worth of ETH, bringing the total staked amount to $96.59 million.
data.description: According to Arkham, the Ethereum Foundation has staked another $46.64 million worth of ETH, bringing the total staked amount to $96.59 million.

data.title: MEXC has fully integrated the USD1 stablecoin and launched Launchpool, wealth management, and zero-fee trading.
data.description: MEXC announced the full integration of the USD1 stablecoin into its platform product system and launched a series of activities. The first phase of the USD1 Launchpool officially launched on April 3rd at 18:00 (UTC+8), allowing users to stake USD1 and share a prize pool of 1,500,000 WLFI. Subsequent activities include: USD1 wealth management products, WLFI rewards for holding USD1, zero transaction fees for USD1 trading pairs, exclusive benefits for new users, and a lucky draw. The activities will continue until May 3rd, 2026, with a total prize pool reaching a maximum of 15 million WLFI.

data.title: Bitcoin's Position Index Declines, Indicating Increased Short Positions
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Axel Adler Jr. reported a decline in Bitcoin's 30-day moving average Position Index to -3.1, down from a local high of +3.0 on March 17. According to NS3.AI, this shift suggests a rise in short positions within derivatives markets. Adler also noted that the Liquidation Oscillator increased to 18.6%, reflecting ongoing forced liquidations on the long side and challenging conditions for a sustained market recovery.
